hint: A → B = NOT(A AND NOT(B)). First invert B to get B̄, then AND with A to get A·B̄ (the counterexample detector), then invert the result. The output is FALSE only when A is TRUE and B is FALSE.
title: Material Implication Gate
objective: Build the logical implication A → B
description: Build the material implication gate: A → B is FALSE only when A is TRUE and B is FALSE — the logical counterexample. In propositional logic, implication is the most fundamental connective: every theorem is an implication. Implementation: NOT(A AND NOT(B)).
